section_title: INTRODUCTION TO OTE PATTERN RECOGNITION SERIES VOL. 19
content:
This is volume 19 in a continuing series of 20 videos for the optimal trade entry pattern recognition series
for the Inner Circle Trader YouTube channel. Our example today is the Dollar Yen on a 5-minute basis chart.
We observe the previous day's high where relative equal highs are present. There's a lot of retail theory
about this being resistance and any shorts' protective stop loss could be 'protected' above these highs. We
will see about that. Remember this is the high. Please pause the video to study the chart before
annotations are shown.

section_title: IDENTIFYING OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY IN THE NEW YORK SESSION
content:
Let's add the annotations now. We have our New York session, which is always static at 8:30 in the
morning New York time to 11 o'clock New York time. You'll see that we create optimal trade entries in here. I
like this one because we've already came back down a little deep on that and then it broke a high. This
aligns with concepts detailed in the scalping or high probability scalping series on my YouTube channel,
which provides more details for trusting bias and additional tips. Let's go back to this example here:
- We have the low, the high, and the optimal trade entry dropping down on the candle at 10:25 AM New
York time.
- Hypothetical entry would be 108.70.
- The entry would have suffered 3 pips drawdown.
- The stop-loss is 10 pips, which takes us at 108.60.

The trade doesn't give us too much at the old high, about 14 pips. At one standard deviation, it offers 20
pips. Finally, it makes it to the 1.5 standard deviation right here on this candle, offering 28 pips. Two
standard deviations could potentially see a pop up in the Asian session, because at the time of this
recording, it's 7:39 PM local time in New York. This concludes the business for this example. Tomorrow is
our last and final episode for this series. If you give me thumbs up in tomorrow's video, it might even inspire
me to do something else after this one ends. I'll talk to you next time, wish you good luck and good trading.
